title: Migration executor result
description: Structured result a migration executor agent reports per docs/migration/orchestration-plan.md 'Per-agent task spec'. One report may cover multiple assigned connectors.

connectors.description: One entry per connector this agent was assigned, in the order assigned.

name.description: Connector name; matches the defs/ directory name and metadata.json name.

status.description: migrated: fully ported, self-verify passes clean. partial: some streams/writes ported, at least one blocker remains (connector keeps its legacy implementation for the unported parts). blocked: no usable progress; legacy implementation stays authoritative.

files_changed.description: Repo-relative paths created or modified for this connector (defs/<name>/**, hooks/<name>/** if any; never a path outside the assigned connector's dirs).

streams_before.description: Stream count in the legacy connector before migration.

streams_after.description: Stream count declared in the new bundle's streams.json (0 for a capabilities.dynamic_schema Tier-3 connector with no static streams.json).

write_actions_added.description: Count of write actions declared in the new bundle's writes.json (0 when capabilities.write is false or writes.json is absent).

escape_hatches.description: Every Tier-2 hook file or Tier-3 escalation used, with justification. Empty for a pure Tier-1 (declarative-only) migration.

file.description: Repo-relative path of the escape-hatch file (e.g. internal/connectors/hooks/<name>/hooks.go, or the native/<name>/ package root for a Tier-3 escalation).

reason.description: Which named hook point / Tier-2 trigger / Tier-3 justification this satisfies, per conventions.md section 1 and section 6's decision tree.

fixtures_added.description: Count of fixture files added under defs/<name>/fixtures/ (check.json + streams/**/page_N.json + writes/*.json).

passed.description: Whether `go test ./internal/connectors/conformance -run 'TestConformance/<name>'` passed cleanly at report time.

failing_tests.description: Named failing conformance (sub)test identifiers, e.g. 'TestConformance/<name>/pagination_terminates'. Empty when passed is true.

blockers.description: Typed blockers preventing full migration. Empty for status: migrated.

notes.description: Free-text context: parity-deviation ledger entries reported for this connector (mirrors docs/migration/conventions.md section 5's meta-rule), anything a reviewer or repair agent needs that doesn't fit the typed fields above.

parity_deviations.description: Optional; populate whenever this migration introduces a documented, ACCEPTABLE deviation from legacy behavior per conventions.md section 5's meta-rule (never data-changing for any legacy-accepted input). Every entry here must also be added to conventions.md's parity-deviation ledger by the reporting agent or a follow-up doc patch.

description.description: What differs from legacy and why (which engine limitation forced it).

verdict.description: ACCEPTABLE per the meta-rule (never diverges for a legacy-accepted input); otherwise ENGINE_GAP, which must also appear in blockers[].

api_surface_endpoints_total.description: Pass B only: total documented endpoints for this API (from the vendor's own API reference), independent of what api_surface.json currently covers.

endpoints_implemented.description: Pass B only: count of api_surface_endpoints_total actually implemented as a stream or write action by this migration.

endpoints_skipped.description: Pass B only: documented endpoints deliberately not implemented, with reasons (feeds docs/migration/coverage-report.json).

path.description: The documented endpoint path (and, where useful, method), e.g. 'POST /v1/payment_intents'.

reason.description: Why this endpoint is skipped (e.g. an api_surface.json excluded.category + reason, or a Pass B scoping decision).

type.description: Per conventions.md section 6's escape-hatch decision tree taxonomy.

evidence.description: Concrete grounding: a file:line reference, an engine grep result, a doc excerpt, an error message — never an unsubstantiated claim.
